prompt: Play this short World 3 continue-versus-fold chain.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button and villain is in the cutoff. Who will be in position after the flop?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The button acts later than the cutoff after the flop, so hero is in position.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Hero is in position here because the button acts after the cutoff postflop.
steps.why_v1: Position still matters because acting later gives hero more information.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same seats, but now villain opened first from the cutoff. Hero holds AJs on the button. Which simple response fits better?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Suited broadway strength plus position makes calling the cleaner response here.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Suited broadway strength plus position makes continuing the cleaner response here.
steps.why_v1: A stronger suited broadway hand can keep going when hero has position and the frame is still manageable.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Keep the same seats and facing-open frame, but now hero holds KTo on the button. Which simple response fits better?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Position helps, but this offsuit hand is still too weak to continue comfortably versus the open.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. This offsuit hand is too loose to continue just because hero has position.
steps.recap_v1: World 3 keeps one simple rule: position improves a hand, but it does not rescue every hand once someone already opened.
steps.why_v1: Position matters, but weaker offsuit hands still fold when the frame gets tighter.
